mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.12% represents an extreme monetary accommodation that compresses discount rates and supports asset prices. This always-on signal predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains near zero.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment at 10.2% is at a cyclical peak. A sustained decline of at least 0.5 percentage points over three months signals improving labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by at least 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: When the trigger fires, if the average 60-day S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Coordinated increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based demand recovery from crisis lows. When both series rise month-over-month by at least 1% and 5% respectively, it signals strengthening consumption and investment that lifts corporate profits and equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month increase > 1% and HOUST month-over-month increase > 5%
failure_condition: If after the joint trigger fires, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of historical instances (using data available up to 2009-11-15).

mechanism_narrative: During the early cyclical recovery regime, three consecutive monthly increases in industrial production signal that the manufacturing sector is expanding from a depressed base. This forward-looking indicator predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as output gains translate into higher earnings.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO posts a higher value than the previous month for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If in regimes with three consecutive INDPRO increases, the 60-day S&P 500 Sharpe ratio is below 0.2 or the hit rate is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the federal funds rate from near-zero levels (by at least 25 basis points in one month) signals a rapid tightening of monetary policy. This unexpected hawkish shift raises discount rates and lowers equity valuations, producing negative short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 0.25% (25 basis points) over a 1-month period
failure_condition: If after the trigger fires, S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances (i.e., no significant negative drift).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in the labor market—declining unemployment and rising payroll employment—provides a strong combined signal of economic recovery. When both series move favorably within a month, it predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days as confidence in the labor market strengthens.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines month-over-month and PAYEMS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: If the joint condition yields a 20-day forward return hit rate below 55% or the average return is not statistically positive at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth consistently reflects consumer spending momentum. This always-on trend signal uses month-over-month increases in RSAFS to predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, as rising consumption supports corporate revenues regardless of the broader economic regime.
failure_condition: If the rolling 12-month correlation between month-over-month RSAFS growth and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is negative for any continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The real federal funds rate (nominal rate minus trailing 12-month inflation) captures the true stance of monetary policy. When the real rate becomes positive, it signals a restrictive policy that weighs on equity valuations. This interaction-driven signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in real-rate-positive regimes, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative at the 5% significance level or the hit rate for negative returns is below 60%.
